title: 10 best herbal tinctures for phlegm

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Indian tobacco tinctures helps with phlegm because of its natural expectorant properties. The tincture's active ingredients, such as lobeline and other alkaloids, help to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the lungs and airways. This can provide fast relief for congestion, coughs, and bronchitis. By reducing the thickness and stickiness of phlegm, the tincture allows it to be easily expelled, promoting healthy lung function and sinus drainage. Regular use of Indian tobacco tinctures may also help to prevent recurring respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Antitussive properties: Indian tobacco tincture helps to relieve coughs by reducing the frequency and intensity of coughing spasms, allowing for easier breathing and reduced phlegm production.

Expectorant properties: The expectorant properties in Indian tobacco tincture help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Indian tobacco tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to chronic coughs and excessive phlegm production.
remedy_constituents: Lobeline: An alkaloid, lobeline can help reduce mucus production and alleviate respiratory issues, including coughs associated with phlegm.
Nuciferine: A quaternary ammonium alkaloid, nuciferine has been shown to possess expectorant properties, helping to thin and clear out mucus, making it easier to expel phlegm.
Lobelanine: An alkaloid, lobelanine can act as an expectorant, promoting the clearance of mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, helping to alleviate respiratory congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of lobeline, a natural alkaloid that helps to break up and expel phlegm.
Seeds: They are used for their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out phlegm from the lungs and airways.
Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and clearing out phlegm from the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh lobelia inflata leaves and flowers in the early morning when they are at their highest moisture content.
Dry the collected plant material in a single layer at 35 degrees celsius for 24 to 48 hours.
Combine 250 grams of the dried plant material with 750 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every 2 days.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with phlegm because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The herbal extract contains thymol, a compound that has been shown to increase the production of saliva and mucous, making it easier to expel excess phlegm. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties help to reduce inflammation in the airways, reducing congestion and coughing. By using an herbal thyme tincture, individuals can naturally relieve respiratory issues and promote healthy lung function.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Thyme tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and expel phlegm.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol, inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can contribute to excess mucus production and phlegm buildup in the respiratory tract.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, which can alleviate congestion and sinus pressure associated with phlegm.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol is a phenolic monoterpene that helps break down and eliminate phlegm by its expectorant and antimicrobial properties, which aid in relieving respiratory congestion.

Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene is a sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and ease breathing, making it easier to expel phlegm from the lungs.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a polyphenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways and promote the elimination of phlegm, thereby alleviating respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of thymol, an essential oil that helps to break down mucus and reduce congestion.
Stems: The stems of Thymus vulgaris are also used to make tinctures, as they contain a significant amount of thymol and other compounds that help to relieve respiratory issues.
Buds: The buds of the plant are used to make tinctures, as they contain a high concentration of thymol and other essential oils that help to reduce phlegm and promote respiratory health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 to 2 pounds of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Chop the harvested thymus vulgaris into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine 1 pound of the chopped thymus vulgaris with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the thymus vulgaris and vodka mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with phlegm because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the respiratory tract. The tincture's expectorant properties also aid in loosening and clearing mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up phlegm. Additionally, licorice root has natural antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections that may be contributing to excessive mucus production. Overall, herbal licorice tinctures provide a natural and effective way to alleviate congestion and promote healthy respiratory function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root tincture helps to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can alleviate congestion and coughs caused by excess phlegm.
Expectorant properties: The tannins present in licorice root tincture help to break down and eliminate mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel.

Soften and soothe mucous membranes: Licorice root's demulcent properties help to soften and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with excess phlegm.
remedy_constituents: Licorinic acid: Licorinic acid, a triterpenoid saponin, helps to break down and expel phlegm by its expectorant properties, making it easier to cough up mucus.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and expel phlegm.

Isoglycyrrhizin: Isoglycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear out phlegm from the lungs and airways by stimulating the production of mucus-thinning substances.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are widely used because they contain high concentrations of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to thin mucus and relieve congestion.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures because they possess expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out phlegm and mucus from the respiratory system.
Flowers: The flowers of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm coughs and reduce irritation in the throat and lungs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with a 2% yield expectation.
Crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powder with 75% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Anise tinctures helps with phlegm because of its natural expectorant properties. The essential oils found in anise, specifically anethole and trans-anethole, work to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and expel phlegm. This can provide relief for individuals suffering from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, or chronic coughs. Additionally, anise's natural antibacterial properties help to combat underlying infections that may contribute to excessive phlegm production.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Anise tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Anise contains compounds that reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and chest tightness associated with phlegm buildup.

Antimicrobial properties: Anise tincture has been shown to have antimicrobial effects against certain bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause respiratory infections and exacerbate phlegm production.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole, a terpene, has expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear phlegm from the respiratory tract.
Apigenin: Apigenin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antitussive (cough-suppressing) effects, which can help alleviate phlegm-related respiratory issues.

Pinoresinol: Pinoresinol, a lignan phenolic compound, has been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat underlying infections and inflammation that contribute to phlegm production.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make tinctures for phlegm due to their expectorant properties, which help relieve coughs and congestion.
Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for phlegm because they contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve respiratory issues.
Flowers: They are used to make tinctures for phlegm due to their antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which help ease coughs and respiratory discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of fresh pimpinella anisum leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine the cleaned pimpinella anisum plant material with 500 milliliters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks allowing the mixture to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids immediately.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterfly milkweed tinctures helps with phlegm because of its unique composition, which contains compounds that have a natural expectorant effect. The tannins and flavonoids present in the herb help to loosen and clear out excess mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel phlegm and reduce congestion. Additionally, butterfly milkweed's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe and calm irritated airways, providing relief for those who suffer from chronic phlegmy coughs.
remedy_properties: Antitussive: Butterfly milkweed tincture contains compounds that help to reduce the production of mucus, relieving coughs and congestion associated with excess phlegm.
Expectorant: The tincture's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and cough up congestion.

Anti-inflammatory: Butterfly milkweed tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, chest, and lungs, which can contribute to phlegm buildup.
remedy_constituents: Cardenolides: These cardiac glycosides help reduce phlegm production by inhibiting the enzyme phospholipase A2, which plays a role in inflammation and mucus production.
Asclepias alkaloids: These alkaloids exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues associated with phlegm, such as coughing and congestion.
Triterpenoids (such as beta-amyrin): These compounds have been shown to possess expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up.
remedy_parts: Roots: As the primary part of the plant used for medicinal purposes, roots of Asclepias tuberosa are used to make tinctures for phlegm due to their concentrated properties and bioactive compounds.
Flowers: Asclepias tuberosa flowers are used to make tinctures for phlegm because of their antitussive properties, which help to suppress coughs and relieve respiratory issues.
Leaves: Leaves of Asclepias tuberosa are used to make tinctures for phlegm due to their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest asclepias tuberosa roots in late fall or early spring when they are most potent and abundant.
Clean and dry the roots thoroughly using a clean water rinse and a sterile drying environment for 7 days.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar for 2 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with phlegm because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb, also known as Echinacea angustifolia, has been traditionally used to combat respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, which often cause excessive mucus production. By reducing inflammation in the airways and throat, Kansas coneflower tinctures can help thin out phlegm, making it easier to expel, thus providing relief from congestion and chest tightness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Kansas Coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as echinacein, which helps to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate congestion caused by excess mucus.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of Kansas Coneflower help to protect the body against free radicals that can contribute to chronic phlegm production and congestion, promoting overall respiratory health.

Expectorant properties: The saponins present in Kansas Coneflower have expectorant properties, which means they help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds help to modulate the immune system, which can aid in reducing inflammation and potentially alleviate symptoms associated with immune system dysregulation.

Iridoid glycosides (such as echinaculin and isolechinaculin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation associated with various conditions, including those that may be related to an overactive immune response.

Alkaloids (such as echinatin and echinacoside): These compounds have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help to regulate the immune system and potentially alleviate symptoms associated with immune system dysregulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are commonly used because they contain high concentrations of bioactive compounds, including alkylamides and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

Leaves: The leaves are utilized due to their rich content of flavonoids and other phenolic compounds, which possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate phlegm-related issues.

Stems: The stems of Echinacea angustifolia are also used because they contain a mixture of bioactive compounds, including glycosides and phenolic acids, which contribute to their medicinal properties and potential benefits in managing phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried root of echinacea angustifolia in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop 1 part of the harvested root into small pieces using a sharp knife or mortar and pestle.
Combine the chopped root with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help in reducing phlegm.
Roots: The roots of Solidago virgaurea contain sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds that may help in reducing inflammation and congestion associated with phlegm.
Flowers: The flowers of Solidago virgaurea contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that may help in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of solidago virgaurea flowers and leaves on a dry sunny day at peak potency.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.

remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear phlegm from the airways by increasing the production of mucus and making it easier to cough up.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Mentha x piperita leaves are commonly used to make tinctures for phlegm due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which help to break down and expel excess mucus.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers by cutting them with scissors.
Dry the harvested mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in a warm place for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 1 part of dried mentha x piperita with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it daily for 1 minute.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.

Expectorant properties: Sage's expectorant qualities help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up excess phlegm and promoting healthy lung function.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the leaves of the plant at dawn when they are fresh and free of moisture.
Combine the dried leaves with a neutral solvent such as grain alcohol.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth to separate the liquid from the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in the fall season when they are fully mature.
Dry the harvested ginkgo biloba leaves in a warm place for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind 1 part of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 parts of vodka in a glass jar.
Seal the jar and let it steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass container and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with phlegm because of its natural ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory congestion. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers from the wild or cultivated source.
Chop the plant material into small pieces weighing approximately 20-25% of the total volume of menstruum.
Combine the chopped plant material with high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:5.
Seal the jar and store it in a dark place for eight to ten weeks allowing for gentle maceration and percolation.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves and flowers, ensuring at least 10% of the plant contains viable medicinal parts.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by gently rinsing it under cold running water and pat drying with a paper towel.
Chop the cleaned plant material into small pieces and combine it with a neutral grain spirit in a clean glass jar to achieve a ratio of one part plant to four parts liquid.

Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily to facilitate extraction, before straining the resulting tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container.
Store the filtered tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, away from direct sunlight and heat sources, until ready for use, ideally within 6 months of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese snakegourd tinctures helps with phlegm because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively soothe and reduce congestion in the respiratory tract. The herb's expectorant qualities help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, making it easier for the body to expel. Heat-clearing property: The herbal formula in the tincture can help clear heat from the body, which is believed to contribute to the formation of phlegm in Chinese medicine, thus alleviating symptoms such as chest congestion and coughing.

Directing downward property: The Snakegourd's "directing downward" effect helps to direct stagnant phlegm downwards towards elimination, rather than upward towards the lungs or head, which can exacerbate respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Effective in reducing excessive mucus production and relieving coughs and congestion caused by phlegm.
Rhyzomes: Rich in expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out phlegm from the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest trichosanthes kirilowii roots and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm, dry place for at least 24 hours before commencing processing.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine one part trichosanthes kirilowii powder with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and stir well.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for at least 6 weeks to allow for maceration and tincture formation.

The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and glycosides, work to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to expel excess mucus and phlegm. Additionally, chickweed's soothing effects on the respiratory tract help to calm coughs and congestion, allowing for a more effective clearing of the airways. This natural relief from phlegm and congestion can provide lasting comfort and improved breathing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chickweed tincture helps reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can alleviate congestion and coughing caused by excess phlegm.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in chickweed stimulate the production of mucus and promote its elimination from the body, making it easier to expel phlegm from the lungs and airways.
Antispasmodic properties: Chickweed's antispasmodic compounds help relax bronchial muscles and reduce coughing fits, allowing for more efficient clearance of excess phlegm and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chickweed plant are often used in tinctures as they contain a high concentration of mucilages, which help to soothe and thin out excessive mucus.
Seeds: Chickweed seeds are rich in fatty acids and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and congestion associated with phlegm buildup.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried stellaria media flowers in quantities of 1 cup for this recipe.
Cleanly chop the stellaria media flowers into small pieces to facilitate proper extraction.
Combine the chopped stellaria media with 80% ethanol in a ratio of 1:5 by weight and let it macerate for 14 days.
Shake the mixture twice daily for the first week, then once daily for the remaining 7 days, ensuring thorough mixing and contact between plant material and solvent.
Filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any solid residue, resulting in a clear and potent stellaria media extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with phlegm because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract. The herb's compounds, such as citral and limonene, work together to reduce inflammation and congestion, making it easier for the body to expel excess phlegm and mucus. remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Lemon verbena tincture helps to loosen and clear phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Anti-inflammatory property: Lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the airways, which can help alleviate symptoms of chronic bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions characterized by excess mucus production.

Antiviral property: Lemon verbena tincture has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses that cause respiratory infections, such as rhinoviruses and influenza, which can help reduce the severity of phlegm-related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots are often used in tinctures due to their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out mucus and congestion from the chest.
Buds: The buds of the lemon verbena plant are prized for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease coughing caused by phlegm buildup.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems at peak growth stage for optimal potency and flavor.
Cut the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 50g of cut verbena officinalis with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool, dark place for 14 days to facilitate solvent extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and store in the refrigerator at 40°f to preserve quality.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with phlegm because of its natural expectorant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried calendula flowers in quantities of one ounce per batch.
Chop the harvested calendula flowers into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped calendula flowers with 80% vodka or grain alcohol at a ratio of 1:4.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar for four to six weeks, shaking daily, to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with phlegm because it has natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that aid in loosening and expelling excess mucus from the body. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves in the early morning or afternoon when they are at their peak potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and combine it with a carrier liquid such as vodka or brandy in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction of the plant's bioactive compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract.
Bottle the tincture in dark glass bottles and store it in a cool place for up to 2 years, using 20-30 drops as needed to help alleviate phlegm congestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed tinctures helps with phlegm because it contains compounds that have natural expectorant properties, making it an effective remedy for relieving respiratory congestion. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and stems at dawn or early morning for peak potency.
Clean and chop the harvested material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine one part of chopped eupatorium perfoliatum with two parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for four weeks, shaking daily, to facilitate optimal extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or coffee filters, then transfer to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian tinctures helps with phlegm because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds. remedy_recipe: Gather dried gentiana lutea leaves and flowers in the ratio of 2:3.
Combine one part dried herb with three parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Place the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks or longer.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles and label them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage tinctures helps with phlegm because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the mucous membranes, making it easier for the body to expel excess mucus. The tannins present in borage also have an antiseptic effect, inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses that can contribute to phlegm buildup. remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Borage tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and breathe more easily.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The omega-6 fatty acids and polyphenols present in borage tincture may help reduce inflammation in the airways and sinuses, which can contribute to phlegm buildup and chronic respiratory issues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried borago officinalis leaves and flowers.
Cut the plant material into small pieces to facilitate drying.
Dry the plant material in a warm and dry environment for 1-2 weeks.
Steep one part of the dried plant material in 5 parts of high-proof ethanol for 2-4 weeks.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for phlegm are liquid extracts of herbs that help to break down and eliminate excess mucus from the body, alleviating respiratory issues and congestion. The best herbal tinctures for phlegm include Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, Ephedra sinica, Verbascum thapsus, Sambucus nigra, Mentha x piperita, and Cinchona officinalis. If left untreated, excessive phlegm can lead to respiratory infections, sinus pressure, and fatigue, negatively impacting daily life, work productivity, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to phlegm that are treated with herbal tinctures are respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic coughs. Herbal tinctures like thyme, eucalyptus, and peppermint help to ease congestion, reduce inflammation, and loosen mucus, providing relief from these conditions. Additionally, herbal tinctures like ginger and turmeric may also be used to treat digestive issues such as indigestion and nausea, which can be caused by excess phlegm or mucus in the body.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with phlegm is a blend of Thyme, Echinacea, and Licorice Root. Thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections, while Echinacea's immune-boosting properties enhance the body's natural defense against pathogens. Licorice Root's anti-inflammatory properties reduce congestion and soothe the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel excess mucus. This combination can be taken in small doses, 2-3 times a day, to promote a healthy respiratory system and alleviate phlegm-related issues.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Frontiers in pharmacology, herbal tinctures containing extracts of medicinal plants such as Siraitia grosvenorii, Lonicera japonica, Platycodon grandiflorus, and Glycyrrhiza uralensis may be beneficial for treating phlegm associated with chronic pharyngitis. The study involved 52 patients in the experimental group and 51 patients in the control group, who were given either botanical lozenges or placebos for 15 days. The results showed that the experimental group had significantly lower total scores of pharyngeal symptoms (3.33 ± 2.33) and signs (2.69 ± 1.59) compared to the control group, indicating the potential effectiveness of these herbal tinctures in reducing phlegm and alleviating symptoms of chronic pharyngitis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with phlegm because it contains bioactive compounds that stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. The tincture's properties help to thin and clear excess mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea has expectorant properties that help to loosen and release phlegm from the respiratory tract, providing relief from congestion and coughs. Its antiviral properties also help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to phlegm production, promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune system, which can aid in alleviating excess phlegm production.
Phenylethanoid glycosides: These glycosides exhibit anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce mucus production, making them beneficial for managing phlegm-related issues.
Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, including excess phlegm production.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are primarily used for their immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing phlegm production.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in thinning and removing phlegm from the respiratory system.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in addressing the underlying causes of phlegm production and inflammation in the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea roots and flowers at the end of the summer or early fall season when in full bloom.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in a low temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers with 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking the jar daily with a gentle motion.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with phlegm because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The herb's active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin, work to break down and clear excess mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, its soothing properties help to calm and comfort the throat and lungs, reducing congestion and discomfort associated with phlegm. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate respiratory issues and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins help to thin and break down phlegm, making it easier to expel from the body, and also have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce congestion.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as licorice acid, have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help to reduce inflammation and promote the discharge of phlegm from the lungs and airways.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids, such as glycyrrhizin, have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to thin and break down phlegm, and also have mucolytic effects that reduce the thickness and stickiness of phlegm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizin, a key compound found in the roots, helps to break down phlegm and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin, which is believed to help reduce mucus and alleviate respiratory issues associated with phlegm.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin, which can still contribute to the tincture's ability to break down and clear phlegm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in early morning or late afternoon when the plant is at its highest moisture content.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dark place at a temperature not exceeding 40 degrees celsius for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle and store in airtight containers.
Combine 1 part of the powdered root with 2 parts of 70% ethanol in a clean glass jar and seal tightly.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures helps with phlegm because of its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory system. The tincture's active compounds, such as eucalyptol, work to break down and thin phlegm, making it easier to cough up. This can provide relief from congestion, coughs, and respiratory infections. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, allowing for smoother breathing and easier mucus clearance.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps break down and clear excess mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract due to its expectorant properties.
α-Pinene: Aids in the relief of congestion and phlegm by reducing inflammation and promoting the expulsion of mucus from the airways.
Borneol: Exhibits expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to thin and remove phlegm, making it easier to cough up.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are primarily used due to their high concentration of essential oils, specifically eucalyptol, which provides decongestant and expectorant properties.
Barks: The barks are used because they contain a higher concentration of volatile oils compared to the leaves, making them more effective in relieving respiratory issues.
Stems: The stems are used due to their ability to provide a balanced blend of eucalyptol and other essential oils, making them an effective option for tincture production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest eucalyptus globulus leaves from mature plants in late spring or early summer when new growth occurs.
Dry eucalyptus globulus leaves in a warm dry place with air circulation for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 250 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 750 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks with occasional shaking of the jar.
Strain the liquid from the plant material through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris tinctures helps with phlegm because of its expectorant properties, which aid in loosening and clearing mucus from the respiratory tract. The tincture's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, have natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that help reduce congestion and soothe the airways. This can provide relief from coughs, colds, and other respiratory issues characterized by excess phlegm production, promoting easier breathing and overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: Helps with phlegm by acting as a natural expectorant, making it easier to cough up and expel mucus from the respiratory system.
Thymol: Helps with phlegm by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, reducing the risk of infections that can exacerbate phlegm production, and by helping to break down and clear mucus.

Rosmarinic acid: Helps with phlegm by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and congestion in the airways, and by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes that can contribute to phlegm accumulation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are used to make tinctures for phlegm because they contain thymol, a natural expectorant that helps relieve congestion.
Roots: Thymus vulgaris roots are used to make tinctures for phlegm because they contain a higher concentration of thymol and other compounds that aid in reducing phlegm production.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used to make tinctures for phlegm because they contain volatile oils that help to thin and expel mucus from the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Combine 250 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers with 750 milliliters of 95 ethanol in a glass container.
Steep the mixture in a dark place at room temperature for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with phlegm because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to break down and clear excess mucus from the respiratory system. This helps to relieve congestion and coughs associated with colds and respiratory infections. Additionally, ginger's warming and soothing effects can also help to calm the throat and chest, making it a popular natural remedy for alleviating phlegm and promoting healthy breathing.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds exhibit expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear phlegm from the respiratory tract.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce congestion and ease the flow of mucus, making it easier to expel phlegm.

Volatile oils (specifically, zingiberene): These essential oils have a decongestant effect, helping to break down and clear phlegm, and also exhibit antimicrobial properties that prevent the growth of microorganisms that can cause respiratory infections.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the primary part used for tinctures due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have expectorant properties that help alleviate phlegm.
Roots: While not as commonly used as the rhyzomes, the roots still contain active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, making them a suitable alternative for tincture production.
Buds: The buds of the Zingiber officinale plant contain volatile oils and other bioactive compounds that may help in reducing phlegm, although they are less commonly used than the rhyzomes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of the dried rhizome root in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the rhizome into small pieces and measure out 1 part of the chopped root per 2 parts of vodka.
Combine the chopped root and vodka in a clean glass jar with a lid and store it in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid tincture to a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid and store it in the refrigerator to prolong shelf life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica tinctures helps with phlegm because it contains ephedrine alkaloids, particularly ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, which have decongestant properties. remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: Ephedrine helps with phlegm by reducing inflammation and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Nordihydroguaiaretic acid (NDGA): NDGA helps with phlegm by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help clear mucus and reduce respiratory issues.
Isoeugenol: Isoeugenol helps with phlegm by exhibiting expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used for its expectorant properties to help relieve phlegm and congestion.
Leaves: Utilized for its ability to thin and clear mucus, making it easier to expel.
Seeds: Employed for its decongestant properties to help reduce phlegm and ease respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ephedra sinica plants by carefully digging up roots and stems during the fall season.
Clean the ephedra sinica roots and stems by rinsing them under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Chop the ephedra sinica roots and stems into small pieces using a sharp knife or tool for easier extraction.
Combine the chopped ephedra sinica pieces with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a 1:5 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every 3-4 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus tinctures helps with phlegm because of its expectorant properties, which aid in loosening and clearing mucus from the respiratory tract. remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help to reduce mucus production and alleviate coughs by exerting an expectorant effect.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Verbascum thapsus are used to make tinctures for phlegm due to their expectorant properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Verbascum thapsus are used to make tinctures for phlegm due to their ability to reduce inflammation and aid in expectoration.
Stems: The stems of Verbascum thapsus are used to make tinctures for phlegm due to their mucilaginous properties that help soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried verbascum thapsus flowers at a time when they are in full bloom.
Combine 1 part of the harvested flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6 to 8 weeks to allow the infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Label and date the tincture store it in a cool dark place and use within 5 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tinctures helps with phlegm because of its natural antiviral and expectorant properties. The elderberry extract in Sambucus nigra tinctures contains sambucol, which has been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses that cause respiratory infections. By reducing the viral load and promoting the clearance of mucus, Sambucus nigra tinctures can help alleviate congestion and coughing associated with phlegm. This makes it a popular natural remedy for colds, flu, and other respiratory conditions characterized by excessive mucus production.
remedy_constituents: Volatile oils (terpenes): These help break down and expel phlegm by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and loosening mucus.
Sambunigrin (phenolic glycoside): This constituent has antitussive and expectorant properties, helping to alleviate coughing and ease the expulsion of phlegm from the respiratory system.
Isovitexin (flavonoid): This constituent exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the thickness of phlegm and prevent it from accumulating in the airways.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties.
Leaves: They are used for their antimicrobial and antitussive properties, which help in relieving phlegm and cough.
Buds: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which help in reducing inflammation and mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the summer months when they are in full bloom.
Dry the sambucus nigra plant material in a low-temperature oven at 100°f for 2 hours.
Combine 1 part sambucus nigra plant material with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth and a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tinctures helps with phlegm because of its natural expectorant properties, which aid in the release of excess mucus from the respiratory tract. The menthol content in peppermint oil helps to thin and loosen phlegm, making it easier to cough up. Additionally, mentha x piperita's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe irritated airways, reducing congestion and promoting easier breathing. By supporting the body's natural detoxification processes, mentha x piperita tinctures can provide relief from phlegm and related respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps to thin and loosen phlegm, making it easier to expel from the respiratory system.
Menthone: It has expectorant properties, helping to promote the clearance of mucus and phlegm from the airways.
Rosmarinic acid: It exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the respiratory tract, making it easier to manage phlegm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their high concentration of menthol, which helps to break down and expel phlegm from the respiratory tract.
Stems: Used due to their rich content of menthone, a compound that has expectorant properties and helps to loosen and clear phlegm.
Roots: Utilized for their robust content of menthol and other bioactive compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and congestion associated with phlegm buildup.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30% of the mentha x piperita plant's above-ground parts on a sunny day.
Chop the fresh plant material into small pieces using sharp scissors or pruning shears.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80-100 proof ethanol in a 1:3 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking daily to ensure extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tinctures helps with phlegm because of its expectorant properties, which aid in loosening and clearing mucus from the lungs. The tannins present in the plant have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and ease congestion. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis contains quinine, a compound that helps to thin and expel mucus, making it easier to cough up. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, by promoting a more productive cough and relieving phlegm buildup.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: It helps reduce mucus production and inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel phlegm.
Cinchonine: This alkaloid helps to break down and thin out mucus, making it easier to cough up and clear out of the respiratory system.
Quinidine: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe and expel phlegm.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cinchona officinalis contain the highest concentration of quinine, an alkaloid that helps to break down phlegm and relieve coughs.
Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis are used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of quinine and other alkaloids that aid in reducing phlegm.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Cinchona officinalis contain a range of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids, that help to reduce inflammation and break down phlegm in the respiratory system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis bark in the early morning after the first frost when the moisture content is at its lowest.
Clean the harvested cinchona officinalis bark by gently brushing off any debris with a soft-bristled brush.
Chop the cleaned cinchona officinalis bark into small pieces weighing 250 grams for a standard tincture batch.
Combine the chopped cinchona officinalis bark with 75 percent vodka and 25 percent distilled water in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar gently every week.
